ksa_text: Ability to identify, assess, and prioritize risks in AI systems and governance contexts; develop mitigation strategies calibrated to organizational risk tolerance and operational constraints.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-A-001 (AI Governance/Ability) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.507, highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.507). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. Data catalog platform management: catalog tooling, metadata ingestion workflows, taxonomy/ontology design, data discovery enablement, and catalog governance.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.507, and AI Governance (AG) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this AI Governance KSA for Data Catalog Manager?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI Governance competencies do not appear as Data Catalog Manager requirements in job postings. They are allocated to specialist roles. Exclusion consistent with market patterns.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld by market evidence. AI Governance is not a Data Catalog Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: KSA AG-A-001 (AI Governance/Ability) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.507, highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.507). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. Data catalog platform management: catalog tooling, metadata ingestion workflows, taxonomy/ontology design, data discovery enablement, and catalog governance.
